name: setup
context: Install and initialize the Sentry SDK for error tracking in browser or Node.js applications
info: This skill covers end-to-end Sentry SDK setup: installing the correct package for your platform (@sentry/browser, @sentry/node, @sentry/react, @sentry/nextjs), calling Sentry.init() with DSN, release, environment, and sample rates, wiring framework-specific integrations (browserTracingIntegration, replayIntegration, Express/Koa middleware), and verifying that errors flow to your Sentry dashboard. The SDK must be initialized as early as possible in your application lifecycle — before any other imports in Node.js (via an instrument.js file) and before React renders in the browser. For Node.js, CommonJS projects require() the instrument file first; ESM projects use the --import flag. See [[sentry/config]] for the full options reference and [[sentry/capture]] for reporting errors after setup.
triggers: set up sentry in my project
body:
# Sentry SDK Setup Sentry must be initialized before any other application code runs. The SDK hooks into global error handlers, promise rejections, and framework lifecycle events to automatically capture errors. ## Browser Setup ```bash npm install @sentry/browser ``` ```javascript // src/instrument.js — import this FIRST in your entry point import * as Sentry from "@sentry/browser"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, release: process.env.SENTRY_RELEASE || "my-app@1.0.0", environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT || "production", tracesSampleRate: 0.2, replaysSessionSampleRate: 0.1, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, integrations: [ Sentry.browserTracingIntegration(), Sentry.replayIntegration(), ], }); ``` ## Node.js Setup ```bash npm install @sentry/node ``` ```javascript // instrument.js — must be required/imported BEFORE all other modules const Sentry = require("@sentry/node"); Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.SENTRY_DSN, release: process.env.SENTRY_RELEASE, environment: process.env.SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT || "production", tracesSampleRate: 0.2, sendDefaultPii: true, }); ``` ### CommonJS ```javascript // app.js require("./instrument"); // MUST be first const express = require("express"); const app = express(); ``` ### ESM (Node 18.19.0+) ```bash node --import ./instrument.mjs app.mjs ``` ## React Setup ```bash npm install @sentry/react ``` ```javascript import * as Sentry from "@sentry/react"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.REACT_APP_SENTRY_DSN, release: process.env.REACT_APP_SENTRY_RELEASE, environment: process.env.NODE_ENV, integrations: [ Sentry.browserTracingIntegration(), Sentry.replayIntegration(), ], tracesSampleRate: 0.2, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, }); ``` ```jsx // Wrap your app with Sentry Error Boundary import * as Sentry from "@sentry/react"; function App() { return ( Something went wrong

}> ); } ``` ## Next.js Setup The easiest method uses the Sentry wizard: ```bash npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs ``` This creates `sentry.client.config.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `sentry.edge.config.ts`, updates `next.config.js` with `withSentryConfig()`, and configures source map uploads. Manual setup: ```bash npm install @sentry/nextjs ``` ```javascript // sentry.client.config.ts import * as Sentry from "@sentry/nextjs"; Sentry.init({ dsn: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN, tracesSampleRate: 0.2, replaysOnErrorSampleRate: 1.0, integrations: [Sentry.replayIntegration()], }); ``` ## Verification After setup, trigger a test error to verify events reach Sentry: ```javascript // Add to any route or page temporarily Sentry.captureException(new Error("Sentry setup verification")); ``` Or throw in the browser console: ```javascript throw new Error("Sentry test"); ``` Check your Sentry dashboard Issues page within 30 seconds — the error should appear. ## Environment Variables | Variable | Purpose | Required | |----------|---------|----------| | `SENTRY_DSN` | Data Source Name for your project | Yes | | `SENTRY_RELEASE` | Release version (e.g., `my-app@1.2.3`) | Recommended | | `SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT` | Environment name (production, staging) | Recommended |

sections.title: Browser SDK setup
sections.body: Install `@sentry/browser` and call `Sentry.init()` at the top of your entry point. Pass `dsn`, `release`, `environment`, and `tracesSampleRate`. Add `browserTracingIntegration()` for performance monitoring and `replayIntegration()` for session replay. The DSN is safe to include in client-side bundles — it only permits event submission, not data access.

sections.title: Node.js SDK setup
sections.body: Install `@sentry/node` and create an `instrument.js` file that calls `Sentry.init()`. This file must be required/imported before any other application code. For CommonJS: `require('./instrument')` as the first line of your entry point. For ESM (Node 18.19.0+): use `node --import ./instrument.mjs app.mjs`. The Node SDK auto-instruments HTTP, `fs`, and database modules via Node's diagnostic channel API. Set `sendDefaultPii: true` to capture IP addresses and request data.

sections.title: React SDK setup
sections.body: Install `@sentry/react` which extends `@sentry/browser` with React-specific integrations. Wrap your component tree in `<Sentry.ErrorBoundary>` to catch render errors. The SDK provides `Sentry.withProfiler()` HOC for component render profiling and `createRoutesFromChildren` integration for React Router tracing. For React environment variables, prefix with `REACT_APP_` (Create React App) or `VITE_` (Vite).

sections.title: Next.js SDK setup
sections.body: Run `npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs` for automated setup. The wizard creates three config files: `sentry.client.config.ts` (browser), `sentry.server.config.ts` (Node.js server), and `sentry.edge.config.ts` (edge runtime). It wraps `next.config.js` with `withSentryConfig()` which handles source map uploads automatically. For the App Router, the wizard creates `app/global-error.tsx` to capture root layout errors. Use `NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN` for client-side DSN exposure.

sections.title: Verifying the setup
sections.body: After initialization, call `Sentry.captureException(new Error('test'))` or throw an unhandled error. Check the Sentry dashboard Issues page within 30 seconds. If no event appears: verify the DSN is correct, check the browser network tab for requests to `ingest.sentry.io`, ensure `Sentry.init()` runs before the error, and check that `sampleRate` is not set to `0`. Enable `debug: true` in init options for verbose console output.

related.note: After setup, use capture methods to report errors manually

scripts.name: Setup Sentry
scripts.description: Install the Sentry SDK and create an initialization file for browser or Node.js

SENTRY_DSN.description: Sentry project DSN from Settings → Client Keys

SENTRY_PLATFORM.description: Target platform: browser, node, react, or nextjs

instrumentFile.description: Path to the created instrument file

packageInstalled.description: Name of the installed Sentry package
